summary: S3 Storage Integration Testing Complete - AWS S3 successfully configured and verified working. All file upload/download routes now use the unified StorageService with S3 (notarychain-documents bucket in us-east-2).

minor.issue: Rate limiting at 10/min causes test failures when running many sequential auth tests

seed_data_creation: Created test document seals, credentials, and notary seals during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: S3 storage integration fully verified. All file operations use unified StorageService.

blockchain_seal_file_upload.details: POST /api/blockchain/seal-file uploads file, computes SHA-256 hash, records on HCS (testnet)

notary_credential_upload_s3.details: POST /api/notary/profile/credentials uploads to S3. Response shows storage_backend='s3', stored_path='credentials/...'

notary_professional_seal_upload_s3.details: POST /api/notary/professional/seals/upload uploads to S3. Response shows storage_backend='s3', filename='seals/...'

admin_login.details: POST /api/auth/login returns access_token for admin user

key_findings.load_dotenv_order_fix: server.py correctly calls load_dotenv() BEFORE importing routes, ensuring AWS credentials available at StorageService init
